# Language Models

## Subtopics
- **Sequence modeling** `P(x₁…x_T) = Π P(xₜ|x_{<t})`; model `P_θ(x_{1:T})` approximates true
- **Training objective**: MLE = minimizing negative log-likelihood `L = −Σ log P_θ(xₜ|x_{<t})` ⇔ cross-entropy loss
- **Teacher forcing**: model conditions on ground-truth tokens during training
- **Decoding** (decoder = causal masking = autoregressive):
  - Greedy: deterministic, pick most probable; repetitive
  - Temperature sampling: `pᵢ ∝ exp(zᵢ/T)`; T→0 concentrates (greedy), T→∞ uniform (diverse)
  - Top-k: restrict to k most probable tokens
  - Top-p (nucleus): smallest set whose cumulative prob ≥ p (adaptive count)
- **KV-caching**: store past keys/values; at step k only compute Q/K/V for the new token. Trades memory for speed (O(N³)→O(N²) attention over the sequence)
- **Fine-tuning ladder**:
  - SFT — train on same cross-entropy objective as pretraining
  - LoRA — freeze base weights W, add low-rank update `W = W₀ + AB`, `A∈ℝ^{d×r}, B∈ℝ^{r×d}`; works because ΔW has low intrinsic dimensionality
  - RLHF — reward model + RL
  - DPO — directly optimize preference pairs (no separate reward model)

## Interview Q&A
??? question "Greedy vs temperature vs top-k vs top-p decoding?"
    Greedy: always the argmax — deterministic, repetitive. Temperature reshapes the distribution (low=sharp, high=flat). Top-k samples from the k most likely tokens. Top-p (nucleus) samples from the smallest set covering probability p, adapting the candidate count to model confidence.

??? question "Why does LoRA work / what does it save?"
    Fine-tuning updates lie in a low-rank subspace, so ΔW ≈ AB with small rank r. You train only A,B (tiny fraction of params), freezing the base model — far less memory and storage, and swappable adapters.

??? question "What does KV-caching speed up and how?"
    Autoregressive generation recomputes attention over all previous tokens each step. Caching past K/V means each new step only computes K/V for the single new token, cutting redundant work (memory cost for compute savings).
